title: Create an installation user profile
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
text: Create an installation user profile To install Uniface, you must use a user profile that has restore authority on the iSeries system. The iSeries restore commands RSTLIB and RSTOBJ are usually shipped with a public authority level of *EXCLUDE . We recommend that you use user profile UNIFSID to install Uniface , and that you give UNIFSID an authority level equivalent to that for QSECOFR for installing Uniface. Consult your iSeries system administrator if this is not possible. After installation you can reduce the authority level of UNIFSID, although it still requires *ALLOBJ and *JOBCTL if it will be used to run the Uniface Router job. Note: In this documentation, UNIFSID is assumed to be the user profile in use. If you use a different profile than UNIFSID, or you do not give UNIFSID the level of authority equivalent to QSECOFR, then the following considerations apply: User profile UNIFSID is the owner of the objects in the installation savefile. If this profile does not exist, the owner becomes QDFTOWN. The operating system does not allow system-provided user profiles (such as QSECOFR and QSYSOPR) to be the owner of Uniface Router job. The user using AS400INS or running LODRUN must have *USE authority for the following CL commands: CRTJOBD , CRTJOBQ , CRTSBSD , CRTCLS , CRTPRTF , RSTLIB , RSTOBJ , CPY , and CPYFRMSTMF . Alternatively, the user must have *ALLOBJ authority and, if AS400INS is used to create a subsystem, *JOBCTL authority. The user using AS400INS or running LODRUN must be authorized to create a directory in the IFS, under / or /QopenSys . To create a user profile for the owner of the Uniface product objects: Use the following command: where Password is the password associated with the UNIFSID user profile. CRTUSRPRF UNIFSID PASSWORD( Password ) USRCLS(*PGMR) SPCAUT(*ALLOBJ *JOBCTL) TEXT('Uniface Product User Profile') Copy Note: UNIFSID was the owner of the Uniface objects when the objects were saved. To properly reestablish Uniface object ownership, the UNIFSID ID should exist before the Uniface product is installed. If you are using a different code page from the default Uniface code page, you must add the additional parameter CCSID( CodePage ) where CodePage is one of the supported Uniface code pages: Search: Column Code page CCSID Filter results... 19 rows found Table 1.
